airports.name: Dubai International
airports.notes: Struck twice (Feb 28, Mar 1 — Terminal 3). Operating at <20% capacity. Severe cancellations. Intermittent closures with incoming salvos. 20,000+ stranded.

airports.name: Al Maktoum International
airports.notes: Near Jebel Ali port (confirmed debris strike). Reduced operations. Not a reliable alternative to DXB currently.

airports.name: Abu Dhabi International (Zayed)
airports.notes: Debris near airport perimeter (1 KIA, 7 injured). Al Salam Naval Base nearby struck. Etihad Towers damaged. Intermittent operations.

airports.name: Sharjah International
airports.notes: Warehouse fire (satellite-confirmed smoke). Mall struck nearby. Reduced operations.

airports.name: Muscat International
airports.notes: ONLY MAJOR GCC AIRPORT FULLY OPERATIONAL. Primary Gulf evacuation hub. Flights to India, Europe, East Africa. UK chartering evac flights. Seat availability extremely limited — book immediately. Temporary closures possible with salvos.

airports.name: King Khalid International
airports.notes: Targeted Mar 1 (intercepted). Flights paused Mar 3 after drone strikes. US Embassy struck nearby. Intermittent operations — unreliable.

airports.name: King Fahd International
airports.notes: Eastern Province under sustained attack pressure. Ras Tanura refinery (nearby) shut down. High-value energy region. Use with extreme caution.

airports.name: King Abdulaziz International
airports.notes: US Embassy shelter-in-place active for Jeddah. Less directly targeted than Riyadh/Eastern Province. Better option within Saudi if necessary.
